title: desmoncus horridus subsp. palustris
intro: Desmoncus horridus subsp. palustris, also known as the 'thorny palm', is a species of palm tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and used in infusions, tinctures, and other concoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
